description: Declarative map (IMP-189): each existing deterministic quality check declares which ISO 29148 characteristics it covers via `covers_29148`. Inert metadata: nothing here executes a check, it only records coverage so the /validate 29148 report can show which characteristics have a check and which are honestly uncovered. A check that maps to no characteristic declares an empty list rather than a forced mapping.

checks.label: EARS pattern conformance
checks.note: classify_ears / not_ears_normalizable signal: a statement conforms to one of the five EARS patterns or is flagged.

checks.label: Ambiguity and weak-word signals
checks.note: ambiguous_term, unanchored_quantifier, and passive_voice signals from score_requirement_quality.

checks.label: Compound-statement (singularity) signal
checks.note: Flags a statement that combines more than one required system action.

checks.label: Missing-verification signal for prose
checks.note: Fires for non-EARS prose lacking any verification, metric, acceptance, or test signal. Does NOT scrutinize well-formed EARS statements — that facet is closed by iso29148.verifiability_anchor.

checks.label: QuRE weak-word smell catalog
checks.note: Bilingual weak-wording markers that name quality/success without a testable expectation.

checks.label: Expected-evidence source contract
checks.note: Checks that a spec unit's declared expected_evidence kind matches its cited sources — verifiability of the evidence trail, not of the statement wording.

checks.label: Mom-Test hypothetical-question warning
checks.note: Elicitation hygiene: flags an elicited question phrased as a future opinion instead of a concrete past event. This is about how a gap is ASKED, not a characteristic of a requirement statement, so it honestly maps to none of the nine.

checks.label: Requirement-statement verifiability anchor
checks.note: NEW rule: flags a requirement statement (including a well-formed EARS one) that carries no measurable or observable acceptance anchor (metric, threshold, acceptance/test signal). Goes beyond expected_evidence, which checks the source trail rather than the statement itself.
